pageHero.title: Behavioral Interview Guide
pageHero.subtitle: The STAR method is your framework. Prepare 6-8 stories that show senior/staff-level impact.

starCards.title: Situation
starCards.description: Set the scene briefly. 15-20 seconds max. No full backstory needed.

starCards.title: Task
starCards.description: Your specific role and what you needed to achieve. Make it personal.

starCards.title: Action
starCards.description: What YOU did (not the team). Be specific about your decisions and steps.

starCards.title: Result
starCards.description: Quantify the outcome. Use numbers: %, $, time saved, scale, team size.

items.text: Conflict resolution - Disagreement with a teammate or stakeholder
Technical leadership - Driving a major technical decision or architecture change
Failure & learning - A mistake you made and how you recovered
Cross-team impact - Working across teams/orgs to deliver results
Tight deadline - Delivering under pressure with quality trade-offs
Mentoring / growing others - How you’ve developed team members
Innovation - Something you built or proposed that wasn’t asked for
Ambiguity - Navigating unclear requirements or shifting priorities

tipBoxes.title: Pro Tips for Senior/Staff Level
tipBoxes.text: Focus on organizational impact, not just personal achievement. Talk about how your work influenced team direction, engineering culture, or company strategy. Use metrics whenever possible: revenue impact, performance improvements, reduced incidents, team velocity gains.

tipBoxes.title: Timing Guide
tipBoxes.text: Keep each answer to 60-90 seconds. Situation: 15-20s. Task: 10s. Action: 30-40s (this is the meat). Result: 15-20s. Practice with a timer.
